name: git-guard-flag-anchoring
doc: Regression for the token-boundary fix (patterns.ts FLAG_END, 0.1.61): destructive flags like `-f`/`--force`/`--hard`/`-D` must never match as a plain substring of a branch/file-name argument.

steps.doc: Live false-positive (proven pre-fix): `-f` inside `guard-false-positives` used to match GIT_BLOCKED's `/git push.*-f/` as a substring, hard-denying an ordinary branch delete. After anchoring, GIT_BLOCKED no longer matches — the command falls through to GIT_ASK's plain `/git push/` (evaluate.ts:35), same confirmation any push gets, not a 'Destructive git command' hard block.
A real `--force` flag (token-boundary, not embedded in a word) still hard-blocks via GIT_BLOCKED — the anchoring fix must not open a hole for genuine destructive pushes.
